output: A cultural-sensitivity and privacy screen specification document committed via PR, reviewed by the cultural/privacy reviewer.
acceptanceCriteria: The spec defines three screen categories: (1) Indigenous/TK materials (TK Label indicators, community-provenance signals), (2) sensitive ethnographic or human-remains content (field/collection signals), (3) living-person personal data.
Detection signals and metadata fields to check for each category are enumerated.
The hold procedure is specified: flagged items are quarantined with a reason, not fanned out, pending reviewer sign-off.
The reviewer workflow is defined: who reviews, what sign-off looks like, and what happens if no reviewer is available (hold indefinitely).
The spec is reviewed by the cultural/privacy reviewer role before implementation.
The spec records that the screen is separate from the rights-gate and must run on every cleared item before fan-out.
objective: Specify the cultural-sensitivity and privacy screen, including categories, detection signals, hold procedures, and reviewer workflow, so items carrying TK Labels, sensitive content, or living-person data are held for review rather than auto-fanned-out.
context: Public-domain status does not mean ethically clear: LoC holds Indigenous materials with Traditional Knowledge Labels, sensitive ethnographic and human-remains content, and items that surface living-person personal data, all requiring a separate screen before fan-out. This screen is independent of the rights-gate. Without it, PD but ethically sensitive items could be fanned into downstream tasks without review. See PLAN.md (Solution approach section 4).
title: Cultural-sensitivity / privacy screen spec (TK Labels, sensitive, living-person)
